selected_items.item: Verify the claimed direct community-grant lineage from Bio/VitaDAO to CrossBridge Bio and the labels/methodology behind the $2B+ / $50M+ ecosystem figures.
Verify current DIEM supply/utilization/holder mix, VVV staking and the relationship between compute liabilities, paid revenue, burns and June-attributed usage.
Verify TAPE and supported node readiness, release/build/config/operator evidence, first-party bridge contracts and Hyperliquid prerequisites.

decision_trail.source: Texas Medical Center, BioNTX, VitaDAO and Bio primary pages
decision_trail.summary: TMC traces CrossBridge to its 2023 CPRIT-funded accelerator, TMC Venture Fund and UTHealth dual-payload ADC technology; BioNTX reports a definitive Lilly deal worth up to $300M. VitaDAO's actual $300K community raise was the same scientist's separate VitaRNA/Artan Bio IP-NFT. Bio's current site labels $50M+ as treasuries raised, $15M+ as funded research and $2B+ as ecosystem trading, without a transaction-level ledger.
decision_trail.decision: disproven — acquisition confirmed, but the direct Bio/VitaDAO grant-to-CrossBridge lineage and altered metric labels are false as stated; close the watch item and reopen only for CrossBridge-specific funding/IP evidence or a published methodology

decision_trail.source: Official Venice VVV/DIEM docs and public APIs
decision_trail.summary: The official API reported 32.994M VVV staked, 37,093 DIEM supply, 29,222 DIEM staked and a 38,000 target. Six complete days consumed 12,753–15,321 DIEM, or 43.1%–52.4% of staked capacity and 47.1% in aggregate. Docs confirm one DIEM renews $1/day of compute. No source attributes requests or revenue to the June app.
decision_trail.decision: confirmed — close the missing current DIEM/staking/utilization-source item with official machine-readable data; preserve burn/revenue reconciliation and app attribution as ordinary KPI/open-question work

decision_trail.source: Official Octra GitHub/docs/RPC, Ethereum Blockscout and Hyperliquid API
decision_trail.summary: The first-party Ethereum bridge is documented with canonical wOCT, EthereumBridge, OctraLightClient and BridgeVault addresses; verified wOCT showed 197,789.585123 supply and 3,350 holders. Public RPC exposed network/account/transaction statistics. The node remains an unsigned internal-use pre-release with no tag, release, license, CI or deployment; validator onboarding is paused, no TAPE-named artifact was found, and Hyperliquid spot metadata has no OCT/wOCT token.
decision_trail.decision: confirmed — close the broad readiness-state check: the bridge is live/documented, while the June TAPE/node schedule is stale and Hyperliquid spot absent; future release/audit/listing events remain catalyst triggers rather than carry-forward verification debt

errors: All three delegated research workers timed out before returning summaries; their live transcripts preserved completed source/API observations, which were independently re-run and verified before editing.

decision_trail.source: Official NEAR X, canonical nearcore releases/code and direct mainnet RPC
decision_trail.summary: Official NEAR announced Protocol v86 live. Direct RPC returned protocol 86, node 2.13.1 and the 100 Ggas ML-DSA verification charge. Release notes and merged epoch config establish FIPS-204 ML-DSA-65 plus automatic epoch-boundary shard splitting with a 40B memory threshold, 10B minimum child size, maximum 10 shards and two-epoch spacing. The RPC's false dynamic_resharding field is the legacy genesis value surfaced by ProtocolConfigView, not the v86 epoch feature gate.
decision_trail.decision: Close the vote/activation question; update the catalyst, KPI methodology, open question, Latest feed, indexes, generated page and changelog. Continue first-split, wallet-adoption and reliability checks.

decision_trail.source: Official Chutes X and Parallax draft technical report
decision_trail.summary: Chutes surfaced a 20B sparse-MoE Parallax run across non-colocated L40S/RTX 6000 Ada composers and remote RTX 4090 workers. The social graphic adds a 5090, two continents, about 6 seconds/step and under $10/hour; the report gives validation-loss point estimates but explicitly says it lacks replicated equivalence tests and measured wall-clock speedups. No code/checkpoint, paid job or SN64 training-economics loop is live.
decision_trail.decision: Advance both Parallax catalysts, add a reproducibility/paid-economics open question, Latest entry and changelog; keep the evidence at technical-demo rather than production-demand status.

Built the queue from the newest scan-debug next_checks and every canonical Unverified Watch Items section, excluded the Grass check because it is not due until July 22, selected exactly the two July 14 checks plus the passed-deadline Targon item that won the July 15 tie-break, and resolved Bankr's missing dashboard as confirmed, Yanez/Bitsec technical follow-up as stale, and TargonOS as confirmed.

selected_items.item: Obtain and reproduce the Robinhood launchpad dashboard, volume, fees and launches; measure concentration/retention and separate Bankr/creator/token routing.
Identify the Yanez/Bitsec vulnerability, delayed release/component, severity, report, patch, retest and any production/client/biometric/fund exposure; verify client and revenue-to-alpha evidence.
After the July 16 E081 deadline, identify exactly what TargonOS shipped, including artifacts, hardware, attestation, availability and any usage/economic evidence.

decision_trail.source: Official Bankr public dashboard/Discover APIs and core-builder snapshot
decision_trail.summary: The public dashboard returned $4.886B all-chain volume, $1.606B Doppler volume, 1.603M deployments, $13.207M labeled Bankr fees and $19.440M creator fees. Its July 8-13 Doppler history sums to $51.043M and 12,149 deployments, broadly corroborating the earlier intra-day $49.307M / 11,265 Robinhood snapshot. Robinhood Discover exposes token-level activity but its 24h sum did not reconcile to the dashboard and it does not provide clean cohort retention.
decision_trail.decision: confirmed — close the missing-dashboard/source item; preserve chain attribution, retention, net-revenue and BNKR routing as ordinary metric/open-question work rather than carrying this verification item

decision_trail.source: Official Yanez/Bitsec sources, app stores and Yanez partner publications
decision_trail.summary: The only vulnerability evidence remains Yanez's high-level quote; no component, repository, report, severity, patch, retest or exposure artifact exists on the checked primary surfaces. Alternate checks confirmed YanezYID v1.0 on Apple and Google Play and official Yanez publications naming Syklo and Nexartis, but none identifies the vulnerable component or proves SN54 revenue conversion.
decision_trail.decision: stale — close the undifferentiated technical-disclosure hunt as non-actionable and not evidence of an exploit; reopen only for a report, code/patch, incident or exposure statement

decision_trail.source: Official Targon docs, signed release server and canonical GitHub repository
decision_trail.summary: TargonOS 0.1.0 is a real live release: the July 17 directory contains a 3.9 GB ISO, netboot bundle, SHA-256 manifest and minisign files. Docs require Intel TDX, TPM 2.0, GPUs and a miner hotkey, say the signed installer pins release/hardware-validation endpoints, and state that every boot attests, unlocks encrypted storage and starts services. It replaces manual TVM provisioning; Tower remains waitlist-only and adoption/audit/utilization/buyback effects are not yet published.
decision_trail.decision: confirmed — close the event-artifact ambiguity as a completed live OS release; move adoption, audit/reproducibility, reliability and economic effects into the catalyst's ordinary watch-next field

errors: YouTube subtitle retrieval was blocked by YouTube's bot check; official event follow-ups, docs, repository and signed release artifacts supplied dispositive Targon evidence without relying on the video.

queue_state.note: All three selected items were resolved and removed. Untouched checks preserve their original carried_from/origin strings. Grass remains explicitly not due until July 22.

scope: Broad scan of all 25 enabled projects, excluding user-suppressed POD. Checked three public intake issues, refreshed 13 configured direct-metric projects while preserving the suppressed snapshot, searched all 94 tracked official/team/core X accounts in ten explicit groups plus a broad high-signal weak-signal query, investigated canonical watch items, and performed the mandatory >7-day substantive non-X review for GEODNET. decision_trail.summary: A final post-fast-forward refresh collected 31 metrics for 13 configured enabled projects at 2026-07-20T12:50:28Z while preserving, not fetching, the suppressed snapshot. Versus the prior broad-scan baseline, Kinetiq TVL moved from $867.1M to $874.9M; no configured materiality rule was crossed.
decision_trail.decision: Persist routine metric refreshes; no standalone metric changelog entry.

decision_trail.source: Canonical GIP Git repository and DefiLlama fees API
decision_trail.summary: Mandatory stale-project review confirmed canonical GIP HEAD remains GIP-13 from 2026-04-15 with no formal GIP-14. Live fees remained $28,344 over 24h, $202,234 over 7d, $904,359 over 30d and $10.401M all-time; no measured GEO-PULSE adoption or recurring-revenue evidence surfaced.
decision_trail.decision: No changelog and no last_updated reset; carry GEO-PULSE adoption/revenue and formal GIP-14 checks.

decision_trail.source: Official MetaDAO X and official sale docs
decision_trail.summary: Official MetaDAO said Credible accepted $4M of $32.775M committed, set CRED at $0.40 / about $9.065M FDV and made CRED plus USDC refunds claimable. Direct HTTPS retrieval of official docs confirmed discretionary caps, time-weighted accumulators, a sparse-pool fill boost, same-price settlement and guaranteed selected-investor allocations.
decision_trail.decision: Update Credible and ownership-launch catalysts, KPI methodology, open questions, watch item, Latest feed and changelog; narrow follow-up to versioned program/code parity, onchain settlement, concentration, fees and META accrual.

decision_trail.source: SrMessi X postmortem and subsequent account activity
decision_trail.summary: The July 1 phishing/Pumpfun account compromise was authentic, but the same-day postmortem says X control was recovered; no MetaDAO protocol or wallet impact and no repeat compromise were established. Residual Pumpfun permission revocation was unresolved in the postmortem.
decision_trail.decision: Mark the watch item confirmed/contained, remove the malformed row from Catalysts, add a low-tier verification changelog entry, and reopen only for renewed or broader impact.

decision_trail.summary: Core contributor everythingempty said the launchpad insists on VIRTUAL-native liquidity pairs despite claiming it could make 10x more revenue without the requirement. This explicitly frames pair demand as a strategic opportunity-cost tradeoff; the 10x estimate has no baseline and does not prove holder cash flow.
decision_trail.decision: Update the mandatory-pairing catalyst, open question, Latest feed and changelog; keep founder conversion, pool depth and actual revenue cost as next measurements.

decision_trail.source: Official/related X accounts via targeted x_search
decision_trail.summary: Targeted verification found no new primary grant record, CrossBridge IP lineage, Lilly transaction terms or definitions for the issuer-reported $2B+ IP-traded / $50M+ science-funded figures.
decision_trail.decision: Keep the existing item partially supported and explicitly carry its verification check.

decision_trail.summary: Derive posted an educational options-exit/RFQ thread repeating venue-liquidity claims; Ownership FM posted a Credible naming/pivot podcast clip; Bankr and GitLawb activity was routine support, transaction or token-launch output. These did not change tracked mechanics or independently verified economics.
decision_trail.decision: Exclude under materiality and routine-core-function rules.

decision_trail.source: Tracked X accounts, prior next checks, canonical watch items, official Git/GitHub surfaces and configured direct sources
decision_trail.summary: No additional official release, governance/tokenomics change, listing/liquidity step change, security incident, funding/team change, high-signal weak item or material configured metric move was established in the scan window.
decision_trail.decision: No changelog; every unresolved prior check is explicitly carried below with origin.

errors: Three delegated broad-scan workers timed out after completing partial API work; ten direct explicit-handle x_search groups plus a broad project-specific query completed X coverage before decisions.

Four explicit-handle x_search groups returned degraded no-citation no-result summaries; they were treated as non-evidence, with adjacent successful account groups, broad X search and official non-X sources used for decisions.
Configured web_extract remained unavailable because Firecrawl is not configured; direct HTTPS, Git, X search and public APIs supplied the evidence.
The MetaDAO Credible app page returned HTTP 429 during direct retrieval; official MetaDAO X and the 200-response sale-docs page supplied source-proximate accepted-cap and mechanics evidence.
The Jupiter token search endpoint returned HTTP 403; CRED onchain settlement remains explicitly unverified rather than inferred from the official claim.

A concurrent direct-metrics commit reached origin/main during the scan. Local research changes were preserved, main was fast-forwarded, the collector was rerun on top of the 09:32 UTC snapshot, and validations were repeated.

scope: Dedicated oldest-first verification only; no broad fresh-activity scan. Built the queue from the newest scan-debug next_checks and every canonical Unverified Watch Items section, excluded items explicitly not yet due, selected exactly the three oldest actionable items (BANKR OKT utility from July 4, CHIP duration/NIM design from July 13, and World Chain WIP-1008 from July 13), and resolved each as disproven, stale, or confirmed.

selected_items.item: Resolve whether OKT gained actual product utility through terminal access gating, x402/Bankr-app payments or governance.
Verify whether the co-founder-mentioned duration/locking element became an official sCHIP/NIM proposal with parameters, contracts, audit or activation.
Resolve WIP-1008's WLD-staked validator-overlay design and distinguish merged specification status from runtime implementation, governance and activation.

decision_trail.source: Official Bankr X posts and current Bankr access/x402 documentation
decision_trail.summary: The official bot's first July 3 response described OKT terminal access, micropayments, premium-feature gating and future governance, but hours later the same bot said OKT had no documented product layer, governance, revenue share, staking, discounts or access rights. Its July 5 response listed x402 acceptance or a dedicated Bankr app as work still needed. Current docs gate apps through Free/Club/Max and document generic custom-token x402 support without any OKT-specific endpoint, access rule or governance artifact.
decision_trail.decision: disproven — close the current-utility claim after 16 days; retain OK, Terminal as a public Bankr app but do not infer OKT access/payment/governance utility

decision_trail.source: USD.AI co-founder X post, official CHIP governance/tokenomics docs and public API schema
decision_trail.summary: The founder's duration/NIM hint is authentic, but current Foundation docs explicitly call staking upgrades exploratory and non-definitive pending governance, security, legal and smart-contract review. They define CHIP-to-sCHIP staking, governance-set cooldowns and a slashable first-loss backstop, but no duration tiers, time weighting, NIM distribution formula, proposal, vote, upgraded contract, audit or activation artifact exists.
decision_trail.decision: stale — close the signal as an impending proposal after seven days; reopen only for an official governance or deployment artifact

decision_trail.source: Canonical World Chain repository, WIP-1008 merge commit and official X accounts
decision_trail.summary: A fresh clone confirms merge commit f43ad2ef added only the 268-line WIP and indexes. The WIP remains status Draft, leaves MIN_STAKE and the StakeManager predeploy address TBD, and says parameters must be fixed before Review. Repository-wide searches found the validator overlay, certificates and slashing definitions only in specification files; no runtime contract, node/sidecar code, audit, testnet, governance activation, release or Foundation endorsement was found.
decision_trail.decision: confirmed — close the weak-signal design claim at the merged-specification level; keep implementation and activation as the existing ordinary catalyst/open question

decision_trail.source: Official staking docs, Bankr bot completion posts and Base Blockscout transaction logs
decision_trail.summary: Official docs specify an 18-hour cooldown and three-day window. After bot-initiated cooldown, a July 17 Base transaction burned 17,911.179970634999489040 sBNKR and transferred 18,484,808.878281170464871296 BNKR from vault 0x019f...17b7 to the user. No unresolved stranded-fund complaint was found. A July 19 bot transaction nevertheless deposited 50,000 BNKR into that same vault and minted sBNKR despite docs saying new deposits are unsupported.
decision_trail.decision: confirmed — close withdrawal reliability as working under real use; preserve the docs-versus-live-deposit contradiction as an open operational question, not a carried weak-signal item

decision_trail.source: percolator-meta issue/commit/repository, GitHub release/deployment APIs and Solana mainnet/devnet RPC
decision_trail.summary: Issue #41 was closed after commit 18477456 added the missing round deadline gate plus regression coverage. The current repo remains experimental/educational-use-only and describes immutable deployment as a precondition; GitHub releases/deployments are empty. Declared TWAP ID TwapBuyBurn11111111111111111111111111111111 returned no account and no signatures on Solana mainnet or devnet. No canonical deployment, upgrade transaction or late-bid incident was found.
decision_trail.decision: stale — preserve the fixed source defect, but close the unsupported live-deployment/exposure follow-up after more than 14 days

decision_trail.source: Kinetiq complete buyback-fill backend, official KNTQ docs/supply endpoint, Hyperliquid API and DefiLlama
decision_trail.summary: The public backend exposes a complete 4,212-fill cache aggregated daily. Through the July 1 post it totals 2,107,173.17 KNTQ and $367,043.92, reproducing the “just over $365K” claim; through July 17 it reaches 2,629,990.48 KNTQ / $440,473.69. Official docs define Markets/Launch/KIP-2/validator buyback routes and the supply endpoint reports 767,385.784 KNTQ burned. kHYPE TVL was $869.9M on July 19, +1.28% from the repo snapshot and below materiality. Official kPoints dates remain September 29/October 1; the formula is explicitly private.
decision_trail.decision: confirmed — close the historical amount/activity claim; keep revenue-source attribution, sKNTQ transfer accounting and dated kPoints execution as ordinary KPI/catalyst monitoring rather than carrying the weak signal

Close the deployment/exposure follow-up for fixed percolator-meta issue #40 by locating a canonical program ID, release/deployment and upgrade transaction.
Resolve the console/test-token incident through a postmortem, mitigation/reimbursement, concrete controls or repeat-complaint evidence.

decision_trail.source: Original X phrase; official MetaDAO ZKFG-008 announcement; co-founder stakeholder/legal updates; Ownership FM resolution report
decision_trail.summary: The original post literally asked ‘first metadao exploit?’ in a thread about the Zinc/ZKFG revenue, ownership and take-private dispute. MetaDAO publicly reviewed ZKFG-008, Proph3t described active stakeholder/legal-resolution work, and Ownership FM later said outstanding revenue was sent without a lawsuit. Searches found no affected program, exploit transaction, drain, security patch or postmortem.
decision_trail.decision: disproven — close the security-exploit interpretation; preserve the underlying Zinc/ZKFG governance and revenue dispute as the already-confirmed catalyst context

decision_trail.source: percolator-meta GitHub issue, fix commit, README, releases/tags/deployments APIs
decision_trail.summary: Issue #40 is closed completed. Commit 11b9637f663935665de54b9b4f9496eb3ef576ab burns proportional shares for both policies while retaining principal-only payouts and adds a LiteSVM regression test. The repository has no releases, tags or GitHub deployments, publishes no canonical program ID, and its README says experimental/educational-use-only.
decision_trail.decision: stale — the source defect remains confirmed fixed, but no affected live deployment, upgrade transaction or exploitation can be established; close the unsupported deployment/exposure follow-up

decision_trail.source: Core-builder X response and complaint thread plus targeted official-account follow-up searches
decision_trail.summary: The builder acknowledged that a team member publicly deployed a test coin during Console redesign, said it should not have happened, denied fee-farming intent, notified all-hands and updated an internal policy document. The complainant reported about $10K of losses. No public postmortem, reimbursement, contract-complete loss reconstruction or repeated incident-specific complaint was found through July 16.
decision_trail.decision: confirmed — close the incident as a bounded acknowledged operational failure; keep the loss amount labeled user-reported and reopen only for documented repeat losses, reimbursement or concrete incident-specific mitigation

scope: Dedicated oldest-first verification only; no broad fresh-activity scan. Built the queue from the newest scan-debug next_checks plus canonical Unverified Watch Items, skipped explicitly not-due items, and selected exactly the three oldest actionable items. Ties were broken by materiality, so the medium-credibility GEODNET item was selected ahead of the low-credibility MetaDAO exploit rumor from the same date.

selected_items.item: Clarify Paradigm/Colosseum backing, ownership language and active support.
Close the deployment/exposure follow-up for fixed percolator-meta issues #38/#39.
Verify the blanket claim that GEODNET's HNT-like model and industry-partner list are not substantive/legitimate.

decision_trail.source: Paradigm official investment registry; Colosseum primary investor statement and official STAMP announcement
decision_trail.summary: Paradigm's live registry lists MetaDAO as a 2024 pre-seed/seed investment. Colosseum calls itself MetaDAO's first venture investor and one of the largest META holders; its official STAMP product gives Colosseum-financed companies a path to a MetaDAO ICO. No primary source found establishes acquisition or operating control.
decision_trail.decision: confirmed — classify Paradigm as an investor and Colosseum as an investor plus active product/distribution partner; close the vague ownership/backing watch item

decision_trail.source: percolator-meta GitHub issues, fix commit, README, releases/tags/deployments APIs
decision_trail.summary: Issues #38/#39 are closed and fixed by commit 87ea552620508ab0f766a82a70ec2e385d7fad0b with regression coverage. The repository exposes no release, tag, GitHub deployment or canonical program ID, and the README still calls the stack experimental and educational-use-only; no affected live deployment or exploitation evidence was found.
decision_trail.decision: stale — source defects remain confirmed fixed, while the unsupported deployment/exposure follow-up is closed and must not be carried without a canonical deployment or incident record

decision_trail.source: Original X claim; GEODNET site; Septentrio and Hyfix primary confirmations; DefiLlama direct fees API
decision_trail.summary: The post names no partner, transaction, screenshot or falsifiable example and received no official reply. GEODNET's live site names established firms; Septentrio independently confirms its corrections-program integration, Hyfix independently confirms ongoing hardware collaboration, and the direct fees adapter returned about $890,203 over 30 days and $10.25M all-time at verification. This does not validate every logo, but the blanket allegation is no longer actionable.
decision_trail.decision: stale — demote the unspecific blanket allegation; future partner/customer claims must be checked individually against named counterparties and direct revenue data

decision_trail.summary: Galaxy frames VVV/DIEM as the most advanced tokenized-inference ownership model, while reporting DIEM is mostly speculative, less than half utilized weekly and a perpetual compute liability; Galaxy disclosed DIEM/VVV financial interests.

decision_trail.summary: Galaxy independently articulates Ambient's paid-job auction and emissions-subsidized verified-inference flywheel, but says trust-minimized buyer demand remains small and final tokenomics do not yet force usage into token demand.
